summary: PII classifier — hybrid rule + ML pipeline at ingestion (replaces legacy post-ingest design)
description_md:
As a compliance-officer, I want the PII classifier to use a hybrid rule + ML pipeline at ingestion time, so that sensitive data is automatically identified and tagged immediately upon entry, reducing compliance risk and eliminating the need for costly post-hoc reprocessing.

**Background:**
This story implements the redesigned PII auto-tagging capability that supersedes the legacy initial design documented in DPLAT/09-pii-tagging-initial-design-LEGACY. The previous approach used rule-only detection running post-ingestion with English-only support, which resulted in delayed PII identification and incomplete coverage for multi-language tenants. The new hybrid architecture combines deterministic regex-based rules with ML-based entity recognition, executes at the ingestion boundary, and supports EN/DE/FR languages out of the box.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
1. Given a data source with customer records containing email addresses, phone numbers, and SSN fields, when data is ingested through the connector, then all PII fields are automatically tagged with appropriate sensitivity labels within the ingestion pipeline before storage.
2. Given a tenant configured for German (DE) language support, when German text containing "Personalausweisnummer" (ID number) patterns is ingested, then the ML classifier correctly identifies and tags these as PII with confidence score ≥ 0.85.
3. Given a dataset processed through the hybrid pipeline, when comparing against the legacy rule-only post-ingest approach, then PII detection recall improves by at least 15% while maintaining precision ≥ 0.90.
4. Given a large ingestion batch (100K+ records), when the hybrid pipeline executes at ingestion, then processing latency increases by no more than 200ms per record compared to the baseline connector without PII classification.

comments.author: dev2@amisol-demo.example
comments.body_md: ML model training is complete for EN/DE/FR. Running validation tests against the benchmark dataset now. Expecting to meet the 15% recall improvement target based on initial samples.

comments.author: qa@amisol-demo.example
comments.body_md: All 4 ACs validated. AC#3 shows 18.2% recall improvement with 0.92 precision. AC#4 latency measurement: +145ms/record. Ready for release in v2.3.
